body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: two-bucket cost-optimizing inference selector
body.description:
**What changed:** inference routing replaced by a two-bucket cost-optimizing selector — time-eligibility filter x difficulty-capability filter -> argmin(dollars) -> failure-bump re-run — populated first with Hex as an owned-local (dollars=0) source.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) the buckets reorder ALL routing (every tier/caller, not just DS/worker) — a mis-set bucket could silently exile a good source or pin every tier onto one box; (2) STALENESS between re-measures (increment 4): if the rolling window is too slow a degraded source keeps its good `time_bucket` for a while — VERIFY increment-4 transitions actually fire and are responsive enough; (3) failure-bump climbing on a NOISY mechanical-failure signal — should terminate cleanly past top bucket (increment-2 criterion), verify it never loops in practice; (4) the `foreground` redefinition could regress latency-sensitive calls; (5) designer/cloud tiers unchanged in intent but reordered in practice; (6) dollars=0 for owned-local makes Hex ALWAYS win its buckets — increment 4 is SUPPOSED to shed load via latency-driven demotion; verify an overloaded (not down) Hex actually gets demoted rather than pinned.
**Signals to watch:** crossing-log source+bucket+dollars distribution — is Hex winning interactive `worker` calls and cloud only appearing on escalation/Hex-down? any tier unexpectedly pinned to one source? escalation-rerun frequency (should be RARE — high rate = bad a-priori difficulty or noisy failure signal); latency regression on foreground calls; `system_alarm` fires on Hex-down WITHOUT wedging routing.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-07-14 or when the selector (increment 2) ships and a day of real routing traffic has logged.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
